'愛知県春日井市東山町2263', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '愛知県名古屋市守山区瀬古', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Matsubara Shrine (Kasugai)}} [[JA:松原神社 (春日井市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11529439}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Matsubara Shrine |画像=[[File:Matsubara Shrine.JPG|270px|]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kasugai|Q26101|WD=Q26101|1=Kasugai, Aichi}}Higashiyama-chō 2263 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}} and others |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} candidate shrine
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Chōnai-sha|1=UNKNOWN}} candidate shrine
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=711|Q23519|WD=Q23519|1=AD 711}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭=Second Sunday of October |別名=Inari Daimyōjin |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Matsubara Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Matsubara Shrine (Kasugai).JPG|thumb|right|150px|Torii of Matsubara Shrine]] '''Matsubara Shrine''' (まつばらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kasugai|Q26101|WD=Q26101|1=Kasugai, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is identified with "Matsubara Tenjin" of Kasuharu District in the '{{ill|lt=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō|Q11041830|WD=Q11041830|1=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō}}', but another theory treats it as a {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for "Takamu Shrine" in Kasugabe District of {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'. ===History=== Tradition says it was founded on {{ill|lt=February 11 (lunar calendar)|Q839324|WD=Q839324|lt=lunar February 11|1=February 11 (Lunisolar)}} of {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=711|Q23519|WD=Q23519|1=AD 711}}), in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Genmei|Q233224|WD=Q233224|1=Empress Genmei}}. Because this area was then called "Takamu," it has been thought possibly to be the "'''Takamu Shrine'''" in the Engishiki registerThere is still a shrine called "Takamu Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Moriyama-ku|Q1155291|WD=Q1155291|1=Moriyama-ku, Nagoya}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}, but it is unknown which one is the shrine listed in the Jinmyōchō.. In the medieval period, {{ill|lt=Inari worship|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari faith|1=Inari Ōkami}} spread. As a result an Inari shrine was merged here as well, and it drew devotion under names such as "'''Takamu Inari Daimyōjin.''' In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, this area belonged to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inuyama Prefecture|1=UNKNOWN}}. In October Meiji 4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}}), Inuyama Prefecture recognized it as the "Matsubara Shrine" in the '{{ill|lt=Kokunai Jinmyōchō|Q11420490|WD=Q11420490|lt=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō|1=Izumi Province Divine Name Register}}'. Inuyama Prefecture then merged with {{ill|lt=Nagoya Prefecture|Q11415214|WD=Q11415214|1=Nagoya Prefecture}} on {{ill|lt=November 22 (lunar calendar)|Q840493|WD=Q840493|lt=lunar November 22|1=November 22 (Lunisolar)}} of the same year. After {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}} was established, in October of the following Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), this shrine became the gōsha of 18 nearby villages and was renamed "'''Matsubara Shrine.''' ===Enshrined deities=== *{{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}} (たかみむすびのみこと) *{{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} (うかのみたまのみこと) *{{ill|lt=Haniyasu|Q9285919|WD=Q9285919|lt=Haniyama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime}} (はにやまひめのみこと) *{{ill|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Wakumusubi}} (わくむすびのみこと) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmiya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (おおみやめのみこと) *{{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} (おおやまつみのみこと) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (さるたひこのみこと) *{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsu-no-o, Nakatsutsu-no-o, Uwatsutsu-no-o no Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} *{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} (じんぐうこうごう) ===Merged / '愛知県春日井市東山町2263', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '愛知県名古屋市守山区瀬古', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Matsubara Shrine (Kasugai)}} [[JA:松原神社 (春日井市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11529439}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Matsubara Shrine |画像=[[File:Matsubara Shrine.JPG|270px|]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kasugai|Q26101|WD=Q26101|1=Kasugai, Aichi}}Higashiyama-chō 2263 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}} and others |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} candidate shrine
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Chōnai-sha|1=UNKNOWN}} candidate shrine
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=711|Q23519|WD=Q23519|1=AD 711}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭=Second Sunday of October |別名=Inari Daimyōjin |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Matsubara Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Matsubara Shrine (Kasugai).JPG|thumb|right|150px|Torii of Matsubara Shrine]] '''Matsubara Shrine''' (まつばらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kasugai|Q26101|WD=Q26101|1=Kasugai, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is identified with "Matsubara Tenjin" of Kasuharu District in the '{{ill|lt=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō|Q11041830|WD=Q11041830|1=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō}}', but another theory treats it as a {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for "Takamu Shrine" in Kasugabe District of {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'. ===History=== Tradition says it was founded on {{ill|lt=February 11 (lunar calendar)|Q839324|WD=Q839324|lt=lunar February 11|1=February 11 (Lunisolar)}} of {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=711|Q23519|WD=Q23519|1=AD 711}}), in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Genmei|Q233224|WD=Q233224|1=Empress Genmei}}. Because this area was then called "Takamu," it has been thought possibly to be the "'''Takamu Shrine'''" in the Engishiki registerThere is still a shrine called "Takamu Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Moriyama-ku|Q1155291|WD=Q1155291|1=Moriyama-ku, Nagoya}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}, but it is unknown which one is the shrine listed in the Jinmyōchō.. In the medieval period, {{ill|lt=Inari worship|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari faith|1=Inari Ōkami}} spread. As a result an Inari shrine was merged here as well, and it drew devotion under names such as "'''Takamu Inari Daimyōjin.''' In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, this area belonged to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inuyama Prefecture|1=UNKNOWN}}. In October Meiji 4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}}), Inuyama Prefecture recognized it as the "Matsubara Shrine" in the '{{ill|lt=Kokunai Jinmyōchō|Q11420490|WD=Q11420490|lt=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō|1=Izumi Province Divine Name Register}}'. Inuyama Prefecture then merged with {{ill|lt=Nagoya Prefecture|Q11415214|WD=Q11415214|1=Nagoya Prefecture}} on {{ill|lt=November 22 (lunar calendar)|Q840493|WD=Q840493|lt=lunar November 22|1=November 22 (Lunisolar)}} of the same year. After {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}} was established, in October of the following Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), this shrine became the gōsha of 18 nearby villages and was renamed "'''Matsubara Shrine.''' ===Enshrined deities=== *{{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}} (たかみむすびのみこと) *{{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} (うかのみたまのみこと) *{{ill|lt=Haniyasu|Q9285919|WD=Q9285919|lt=Haniyama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime}} (はにやまひめのみこと) *{{ill|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Wakumusubi}} (わくむすびのみこと) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmiya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (おおみやめのみこと) *{{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} (おおやまつみのみこと) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (さるたひこのみこと) *{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsu-no-o, Nakatsutsu-no-o, Uwatsutsu-no-o no Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} *{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} (じんぐうこうごう) ===Merged / '愛知県春日井市東山町2263', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '愛知県名古屋市守山区瀬古', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Matsubara Shrine (Kasugai)}} [[JA:松原神社 (春日井市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11529439}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Matsubara Shrine |画像=[[File:Matsubara Shrine.JPG|270px|]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kasugai|Q26101|WD=Q26101|1=Kasugai, Aichi}}Higashiyama-chō 2263 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}} and others |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} candidate shrine
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Chōnai-sha|1=UNKNOWN}} candidate shrine
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=711|Q23519|WD=Q23519|1=AD 711}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭=Second Sunday of October |別名=Inari Daimyōjin |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Matsubara Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Matsubara Shrine (Kasugai).JPG|thumb|right|150px|Torii of Matsubara Shrine]] '''Matsubara Shrine''' (まつばらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kasugai|Q26101|WD=Q26101|1=Kasugai, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is identified with "Matsubara Tenjin" of Kasuharu District in the '{{ill|lt=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō|Q11041830|WD=Q11041830|1=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō}}', but another theory treats it as a {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for "Takamu Shrine" in Kasugabe District of {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'. ===History=== Tradition says it was founded on {{ill|lt=February 11 (lunar calendar)|Q839324|WD=Q839324|lt=lunar February 11|1=February 11 (Lunisolar)}} of {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=711|Q23519|WD=Q23519|1=AD 711}}), in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Genmei|Q233224|WD=Q233224|1=Empress Genmei}}. Because this area was then called "Takamu," it has been thought possibly to be the "'''Takamu Shrine'''" in the Engishiki registerThere is still a shrine called "Takamu Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Moriyama-ku|Q1155291|WD=Q1155291|1=Moriyama-ku, Nagoya}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}, but it is unknown which one is the shrine listed in the Jinmyōchō.. In the medieval period, {{ill|lt=Inari worship|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari faith|1=Inari Ōkami}} spread. As a result an Inari shrine was merged here as well, and it drew devotion under names such as "'''Takamu Inari Daimyōjin.''' In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, this area belonged to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inuyama Prefecture|1=UNKNOWN}}. In October Meiji 4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}}), Inuyama Prefecture recognized it as the "Matsubara Shrine" in the '{{ill|lt=Kokunai Jinmyōchō|Q11420490|WD=Q11420490|lt=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō|1=Izumi Province Divine Name Register}}'. Inuyama Prefecture then merged with {{ill|lt=Nagoya Prefecture|Q11415214|WD=Q11415214|1=Nagoya Prefecture}} on {{ill|lt=November 22 (lunar calendar)|Q840493|WD=Q840493|lt=lunar November 22|1=November 22 (Lunisolar)}} of the same year. After {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}} was established, in October of the following Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), this shrine became the gōsha of 18 nearby villages and was renamed "'''Matsubara Shrine.''' ===Enshrined deities=== *{{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}} (たかみむすびのみこと) *{{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} (うかのみたまのみこと) *{{ill|lt=Haniyasu|Q9285919|WD=Q9285919|lt=Haniyama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime}} (はにやまひめのみこと) *{{ill|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Wakumusubi}} (わくむすびのみこと) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmiya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (おおみやめのみこと) *{{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} (おおやまつみのみこと) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (さるたひこのみこと) *{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsu-no-o, Nakatsutsu-no-o, Uwatsutsu-no-o no Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} *{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} (じんぐうこうごう) ===Merged / precinct shrines=== [[File:Matsubara Shrine (Kasugai) 5.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Shiroko Yoshikane Shrine]] *Shiroko Yoshikane Shrine, others === Nearby === *{{ill|lt=Kasugai '愛知県春日井市東山町2263', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '愛知県名古屋市守山区瀬古', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Matsubara Shrine (Kasugai)}} [[JA:松原神社 (春日井市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11529439}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Matsubara Shrine |画像=[[File:Matsubara Shrine.JPG|270px|]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kasugai|Q26101|WD=Q26101|1=Kasugai, Aichi}}Higashiyama-chō 2263 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}} and others |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} candidate shrine
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Chōnai-sha|1=UNKNOWN}} candidate shrine
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=711|Q23519|WD=Q23519|1=AD 711}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭=Second Sunday of October |別名=Inari Daimyōjin |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Matsubara Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Matsubara Shrine (Kasugai).JPG|thumb|right|150px|Torii of Matsubara Shrine]] '''Matsubara Shrine''' (まつばらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kasugai|Q26101|WD=Q26101|1=Kasugai, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is identified with "Matsubara Tenjin" of Kasuharu District in the '{{ill|lt=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō|Q11041830|WD=Q11041830|1=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō}}', but another theory treats it as a {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for "Takamu Shrine" in Kasugabe District of {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'. ===History=== Tradition says it was founded on {{ill|lt=February 11 (lunar calendar)|Q839324|WD=Q839324|lt=lunar February 11|1=February 11 (Lunisolar)}} of {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=711|Q23519|WD=Q23519|1=AD 711}}), in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Genmei|Q233224|WD=Q233224|1=Empress Genmei}}. Because this area was then called "Takamu," it has been thought possibly to be the "'''Takamu Shrine'''" in the Engishiki registerThere is still a shrine called "Takamu Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Moriyama-ku|Q1155291|WD=Q1155291|1=Moriyama-ku, Nagoya}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}, but it is unknown which one is the shrine listed in the Jinmyōchō.. In the medieval period, {{ill|lt=Inari worship|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari faith|1=Inari Ōkami}} spread. As a result an Inari shrine was merged here as well, and it drew devotion under names such as "'''Takamu Inari Daimyōjin.''' In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, this area belonged to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inuyama Prefecture|1=UNKNOWN}}. In October Meiji 4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}}), Inuyama Prefecture recognized it as the "Matsubara Shrine" in the '{{ill|lt=Kokunai Jinmyōchō|Q11420490|WD=Q11420490|lt=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō|1=Izumi Province Divine Name Register}}'. Inuyama Prefecture then merged with {{ill|lt=Nagoya Prefecture|Q11415214|WD=Q11415214|1=Nagoya Prefecture}} on {{ill|lt=November 22 (lunar calendar)|Q840493|WD=Q840493|lt=lunar November 22|1=November 22 (Lunisolar)}} of the same year. After {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}} was established, in October of the following Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), this shrine became the gōsha of 18 nearby villages and was renamed "'''Matsubara Shrine.''' ===Enshrined deities=== *{{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}} (たかみむすびのみこと) *{{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} (うかのみたまのみこと) *{{ill|lt=Haniyasu|Q9285919|WD=Q9285919|lt=Haniyama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime}} (はにやまひめのみこと) *{{ill|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Wakumusubi}} (わくむすびのみこと) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmiya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (おおみやめのみこと) *{{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} (おおやまつみのみこと) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (さるたひこのみこと) *{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsu-no-o, Nakatsutsu-no-o, Uwatsutsu-no-o no Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} *{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} (じんぐうこうごう) ===Merged / Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kasugai|Q26101|qid=Q26101|1=Kasugai, Aichi}}Higashiyama-chō 2263 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}} and others |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} candidate shrine
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Chōnai-sha|1=UNKNOWN}} candidate shrine
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|qid=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=711|Q23519|qid=Q23519|1=AD 711}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭=Second Sunday of October |別名=Inari Daimyōjin |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Matsubara Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Matsubara Shrine (Kasugai).JPG|thumb|right|150px|Torii of Matsubara Shrine]] '''Matsubara Shrine''' (まつばらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kasugai|Q26101|qid=Q26101|1=Kasugai, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is identified with "Matsubara Tenjin" of Kasuharu District in the '{{ill|lt=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō|Q11041830|qid=Q11041830|1=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō}}', but another theory treats it as a {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for "Takamu Shrine" in Kasugabe District of {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|qid=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'. ===History=== Tradition says it was founded on {{ill|lt=February 11 (lunar calendar)|Q839324|qid=Q839324|lt=lunar February 11|1=February 11 (Lunisolar)}} of {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|qid=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=711|Q23519|qid=Q23519|1=AD 711}}), in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Genmei|Q233224|qid=Q233224|1=Empress Genmei}}. Because this area was then called "Takamu," it has been thought possibly to be the "'''Takamu Shrine'''" in the Engishiki registerThere is still a shrine called "Takamu Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Moriyama-ku|Q1155291|qid=Q1155291|1=Moriyama-ku, Nagoya}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|qid=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}, but it is unknown which one is the shrine listed in the Jinmyōchō.. In the medieval period, {{ill|lt=Inari worship|Q719665|qid=Q719665|lt=Inari faith|1=Inari Ōkami}} spread. As a result an Inari shrine was merged here as well, and it drew devotion under names such as "'''Takamu Inari Daimyōjin.''' In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, this area belonged to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inuyama Prefecture|1=UNKNOWN}}. In October Meiji 4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|qid=Q7746|1=1871}}), Inuyama Prefecture recognized it as the "Matsubara Shrine" in the '{{ill|lt=Kokunai Jinmyōchō|Q11420490|qid=Q11420490|lt=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō|1=Izumi Province Divine Name Register}}'. Inuyama Prefecture then merged with {{ill|lt=Nagoya Prefecture|Q11415214|qid=Q11415214|1=Nagoya Prefecture}} on {{ill|lt=November 22 (lunar calendar)|Q840493|qid=Q840493|lt=lunar November 22|1=November 22 (Lunisolar)}} of the same year. After {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}} was established, in October of the following Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}}), this shrine became the gōsha of 18 nearby villages and was renamed "'''Matsubara Shrine.''' ===Enshrined deities=== *{{ill|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}} (たかみむすびのみこと)
